checks.question: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?

findings.detail: Checked the repository against the milestone brief and found that both prerequisite foundation pieces already exist in this branch: `zustand` is already listed in [package.json](/workspace/reigh-app/package.json:62) and the shared factory plus baseline tests already live in [createEntityStore.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts:1) and [createEntityStore.test.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/state/createEntityStore.test.ts:1). The revision correctly pivots M1 toward finalizing and reshaping that existing foundation instead of adding a second copy.

Checked the repo for the milestone source document named in the brief and only found [docs/structure_detail/settings_system.md](/workspace/reigh-app/docs/structure_detail/settings_system.md:1); the referenced `docs/state_management_refactor_plan.md` is not present anywhere under `/workspace/reigh-app`. That means the plan can only be validated against the prompt text, not against a repository document the executor can re-open, which is still a verifiability gap for an implementation milestone tied to a named doc.

checks.question: Are the proposed changes technically correct?

findings.detail: Re-checked the two previously-blocking contract points against [useAutoSaveSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/settings/hooks/useAutoSaveSettings.ts:431) and [useAutoSaveSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/settings/hooks/useAutoSaveSettings.ts:459). The revision now keeps `initializeFrom` as a React Query-mode no-op and explicitly moves the clean baseline on `reset(newDefaults?)`, so it resolves the earlier contract drift around no-op initialization and clean-after-reset behavior.

Checked the adapter edge cases against the actual hook signature in [useJoinClipsSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsSettings.ts:9), its null/undefined tests in [useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/__tests__/useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts:68), and the string-only factory API in [createEntityStore.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts:31). The plan says nullish `projectId` must still produce idle/defaults/no-op behavior, but it never explains how that is implemented without either calling `useEntity` conditionally or inventing a sentinel entity ID, so one real runtime edge remains underspecified for the migrated adapter.

checks.question: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?

findings.detail: Searched the Join Clips surface in [JoinClipsPage.tsx](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx:248), [useClipManager.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useClipManager.ts:32), and [useJoinClipsPageHelpers.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/pages/hooks/useJoinClipsPageHelpers.ts:13). The migration target is still intentionally narrow: there is one production consumer hook and a small set of callers that read its returned object, so limiting M1 to `useJoinClipsSettings` remains consistent with the actual repo.

Re-checked the earlier double-read concern against the revised steps and the existing merged-settings path in [useToolSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/hooks/settings/useToolSettings.ts:163). The plan now explicitly keeps `useToolSettings` as the only authoritative merged-read source for Join Clips and demotes the store `load()` path to neutral entity bootstrap, which contains the first consumer to one merged-settings runtime instead of two.

checks.question: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?

findings.detail: Checked the supporting infrastructure around the named edit points: the existing shared store file and tests are [createEntityStore.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts:1) and [createEntityStore.test.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/state/createEntityStore.test.ts:1); the Join Clips adapter is [useJoinClipsSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsSettings.ts:1); the persistence/cache boundary is [useToolSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/hooks/settings/useToolSettings.ts:1); and the flush/query behavior that the adapter is replacing lives in [useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/settings/hooks/useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts:66). The revised plan now touches the real glue points instead of pretending this is just a one-file hook swap.

Searched for current `createEntityStore` consumers with `rg -n "createEntityStore\(" src` and found no production call sites beyond the factory definition itself. That means the plan does not miss any existing adopter that would also need migration work in this milestone; the only required integration surface is the new Join Clips adapter plus its tests.

checks.question: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?

findings.detail: Checked all current `useJoinClipsSettings(...)` call sites in [JoinClipsPage.tsx](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx:248) and its tests in [useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/__tests__/useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts:52). Callers only pass either a selected project ID string or a nullish value, and downstream consumers primarily read `settings`, `status`, `updateField`, and `updateFields`, so preserving the public adapter surface instead of exposing the raw store view is still the right direction.

Checked the caller-sensitive contract around nullish arguments against [useJoinClipsSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsSettings.ts:9) and the existing tests for `null` and `undefined` in [useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/__tests__/useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts:68). The revision still needs an explicit adapter rule for these callers because the factory API only accepts `string` entity IDs; without that bridge, the executor could accidentally allocate a fake shared entity or violate the Rules of Hooks while trying to preserve the current idle/no-op behavior.

flags.concern: Join Clips adapter: the revised plan preserves null/undefined `projectId` behavior at a high level, but it still does not specify how a `string | null | undefined` public hook is safely bridged onto `createEntityStore.useEntity(entityId: string)` without a sentinel entity or conditional hook call.
flags.evidence: Current callers and tests pass nullish values in src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsSettings.ts:9 and src/tools/join-clips/hooks/__tests__/useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts:68, while the factory surface is string-only in src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts:31.

flags.concern: Plan source verifiability: the repository does not contain the referenced `docs/state_management_refactor_plan.md`, so milestone-to-repo traceability still depends on the prompt rather than a repo document the executor can inspect.
flags.evidence: A repository search found only docs/structure_detail/settings_system.md and no docs/state_management_refactor_plan.md under /workspace/reigh-app.
